# Strait

> Open-source job orchestration for background jobs, scheduled work, private workers, and workflows.

Strait coordinates work that runs on your infrastructure. It handles triggers, retries, schedules, workflow steps, approvals, live updates, and `dead_letter` recovery.

## Documentation

- [Introduction](https://docs.strait.dev/introduction): What Strait does and when to use it
- [Choose Your Path](https://docs.strait.dev/choose-your-path): Cloud or self-host setup
- [Quickstart](https://docs.strait.dev/quickstart): Run Strait locally and trigger a job
- [Create a Production Job](https://docs.strait.dev/guides/production-job): Add retries, limits, idempotency, and failure handling

## Use Cases

- [Background Jobs](https://docs.strait.dev/use-cases/background-jobs)
- [Scheduled Work](https://docs.strait.dev/use-cases/scheduled-work)
- [Multi-Step Workflows](https://docs.strait.dev/use-cases/multi-step-workflows)
- [Private Network Jobs](https://docs.strait.dev/use-cases/private-network-jobs)
- [Replay Failed Work](https://docs.strait.dev/use-cases/replay-failed-work)

- [Jobs](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/jobs)
- [Runs](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/runs)
- [Workflows](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/workflows)
- [Retry Strategies](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/retry-strategies)
- [Scheduling](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/scheduling)
- [Event Triggers](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/event-triggers)
- [Execution Modes](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/execution-modes)
- [Versioning](https://docs.strait.dev/concepts/versioning)
